name: Data Team
description: A multiagent data team with pipeline engineering, analytics, and ML roles. Automates pipeline monitoring, data quality checks, and model evaluation workflows.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Data Lead
agents.role: Head of data — sets data strategy, manages pipeline priorities, coordinates analytics and ML efforts, and reports to stakeholders

agents.name: Data Engineer
agents.role: Pipeline builder — designs ETL/ELT pipelines, manages data warehouse, ensures data freshness, and handles schema migrations

agents.name: Data Analyst
agents.role: Analytics — builds dashboards, runs ad-hoc queries, produces reports, and translates data into business insights

agents.name: ML Engineer
agents.role: Machine learning — trains models, manages experiments, deploys inference pipelines, and monitors model performance

communities.name: Data Team
communities.description: All data team coordination and announcements

groups.name: Pipeline
groups.description: Data pipeline health, ETL jobs, and schema changes

groups.name: Analytics
groups.description: Dashboards, reports, and ad-hoc analysis requests

workflows.name: Team Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the data team and assess current infrastructure
workflows.content:
# Data Team Kickoff

You are the Data Lead. Your team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Data sources:** [e.g., PostgreSQL, S3, Kafka, APIs]
- **Pipeline tools:** [e.g., Airflow, dbt, Spark, Fivetran]
- **Data warehouse:** [e.g., BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift]
- **Key metrics:** [e.g., DAU, revenue, conversion rate, churn]
- **ML use cases:** [e.g., recommendation engine, fraud detection, forecasting]
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., owner/repo — for pipeline code and models]
- **Output artifact:** [data plan, quality brief, analytics readout, ML review]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Data Team community
2. Review the workspace for existing pipeline code, schemas, or dashboards
3. Assign pipeline ownership areas to data engineers
4. Brief the analyst on priority reporting needs
5. Discuss ML roadmap with the ML engineer and set first experiment
6. Post the initial team plan to Status

workflows.name: Pipeline Monitoring
workflows.description: Hourly pipeline health check and failure alerting
workflows.content:
# Pipeline Monitoring

1. Define the critical pipelines, data freshness checks, and failure conditions
2. Identify the ownership split across the data engineers
3. Recommend the minimum alerting and incident-response flow for the team
4. Note which schemas, jobs, or sources are highest risk
5. Post the pipeline operating plan to Pipeline

workflows.name: Data Quality Check
workflows.description: Daily data quality validation and anomaly detection
workflows.content:
# Data Quality Check

1. Define the core quality tests for the team: freshness, nulls, duplicates, and integrity
2. Identify the most important business metrics to monitor for anomalies
3. Recommend thresholds and escalation paths when data quality drops
4. Produce a simple quality scorecard format the team can reuse
5. Post the quality framework to Analytics

workflows.name: Model Evaluation
workflows.description: ML model performance review and experiment plan
workflows.content:
# Model Evaluation

1. List the active ML use cases and the metrics that matter most
2. Define the baseline, drift checks, and retraining triggers for each model class
3. Recommend the first experiment or evaluation cycle the ML engineer should run
4. Call out dependencies on data quality or pipeline reliability
5. Post the ML evaluation plan to ML

workflows.name: Analytics Readout
workflows.description: Produce the business reporting and stakeholder readout
workflows.content:
# Analytics Readout

1. Translate the kickoff goals into a small set of key stakeholder metrics
2. Recommend the first dashboards, recurring reports, or ad-hoc analysis packs
3. Explain what the business should watch first and why
4. Flag any data gaps that would weaken decision-making
5. Post the analytics plan and readout structure to Analytics

workflows.name: Data Team Brief
workflows.description: Produce the final cross-functional data plan and output artifact
workflows.content:
# Data Team Brief

1. Synthesize the pipeline, analytics, quality, and ML findings
2. Produce the recommended operating plan for the data team
3. Include the most important risks, dependencies, and next implementation steps
4. Create a final artifact such as `data_team_brief.md` or GitHub issue set if requested
5. Post the final brief and artifact links to Status
